subject: Anthropology
title: Homo Floresiensis
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Homo Floresiensis.

hook.text: In 2003, scientists found a tiny human skeleton on an Indonesian island. Three feet tall. Nicknamed the Hobbit.

buildup.text: Homo floresiensis lived on Flores Island until about 50,000 years ago, with a brain the size of a grapefruit.

discovery.text: Despite their small brains, they made stone tools and hunted dwarf elephants. Size doesn't equal intelligence.

twist.text: Island dwarfism may explain their size. Limited resources on islands can shrink large species over generations.

climax.text: Floresiensis proves that human evolution wasn't one march toward bigger bodies and brains. It branched wildly.

quiz.question: What is notable about Homo floresiensis?
options: They were very small and lived on an island
